behaviors.description: Rows with missing required fields are rejected and appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number.
behaviors.justification:
MOCK-MISS: Failure is at module handoff (CSV parser -> validator -> DLQ writer with row metadata), not a single-function rule check. REAL-INFRA: No real OS/network/subprocess is required to prove this behavior; in-process components can reproduce it. MOCK-HIDE: A pure unit test of validator would miss row-number propagation and DLQ payload wiring bugs.
Slot A (evidence quote): "rejected and appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number".
Slot B (boundary signal): Requires coordination across validation outcome, row indexing, and DLQ enqueue payload.
Slot C (adjacent-lower miss-proof): Unit-only validator tests would pass even if DLQ writer drops/rewrites row_number.
Final label rationale: Integration is required by Slot B and Slot C.
INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) No real OS/network/subprocess required by behavior text. (b) No AI/ML output dependency. (c) No multi-agentic chain. (d) No LLM-stage orchestration.
REJECTION-GATE: Rejected System because behavior text does not require real queue transport semantics, only correct local handoff to DLQ with row number.
behaviors.test_description: Wire parser, schema validator, and DLQ adapter in-process. Feed a CSV fixture with missing required fields at known line numbers; assert invalid rows are absent from normalized output and present in DLQ records with exact original row numbers.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test exercises cross-module handoff, which cannot be guaranteed by a lower Unit-only test.

behaviors.description: The transformation correctly converts date strings in multiple formats (ISO 8601, US, European) to a single canonical format.
behaviors.justification:
MOCK-MISS: Behavior is local conversion logic (format parsing/normalization) and does not require module interaction to fail. REAL-INFRA: No OS/network/subprocess needed. MOCK-HIDE: Mocks do not add signal; table-driven inputs catch true failures here.
REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Integration because behavior text focuses on conversion correctness itself, not inter-module handoff or persistence.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven tests for the date-normalization function using ISO 8601, US, and European samples (plus ambiguous/invalid edge cases). Assert exact canonical output (or explicit rejection) per input.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Assertions directly target the single transformation boundary, matching Unit scope.

behaviors.description: A CSV file with 500,000 rows completes processing within the configured timeout without running out of memory.
behaviors.justification:
MOCK-MISS: Real failure emerges from end-to-end runtime behavior (streaming/chunking/backpressure), not isolated function logic. REAL-INFRA: Timeout and memory exhaustion are OS/runtime-level effects; in-process fakes cannot faithfully reproduce process memory pressure. MOCK-HIDE: Mocked data sources/sinks can mask buffering and allocation spikes.
Slot A (evidence quote): "500,000 rows" and "within the configured timeout without running out of memory".
Slot B (boundary signal): Requires full pipeline execution under real runtime constraints (scheduler, GC/memory limits, wall-clock timeout).
Slot C (adjacent-lower miss-proof): Integration with fakes can pass while production process OOMs or breaches timeout due to actual I/O and buffering patterns.
Final label rationale: System is required by Slot B and Slot C.
REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Integration because behavior explicitly asserts process-level timeout/memory characteristics.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline as a real process against a generated 500k-row CSV and real configured timeout/memory limits; monitor completion time and peak memory, and fail on timeout or OOM.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test explicitly depends on process-level performance characteristics that lower levels cannot validate.

behaviors.description: When the S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error, the pipeline retries with exponential backoff rather than failing the entire batch.
behaviors.justification:
MOCK-MISS: Failure often occurs at HTTP/client/SDK boundary (status mapping, retryable classification, timing behavior). REAL-INFRA: Behavior references real HTTP 503 from S3; retry/backoff correctness is sensitive to actual request/response handling and timing. MOCK-HIDE: Mock client methods can always raise a curated error shape, hiding real transport/status-path bugs.
Slot A (evidence quote): "S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error" and "retries with exponential backoff".
Slot B (boundary signal): Requires network client + retry policy + batch controller interaction under actual HTTP failure responses.
Slot C (adjacent-lower miss-proof): Integration stubs may pass even if real SDK does not classify/propagate 503 as retryable in production path.
Final label rationale: System is required by Slot B and Slot C.
REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Integration because behavior text is explicitly about real S3 503 handling, where mock error shapes can hide failures.
behaviors.test_description: Execute pipeline against an HTTP endpoint (or S3-compatible test target) that returns transient 503 responses before success; assert retry count and exponentially increasing delays, and confirm batch ultimately continues instead of terminal failure.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires real HTTP failure semantics and retry timing, which lower levels can misrepresent.

behaviors.description: Rows successfully written to PostgreSQL are not duplicated if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch.
behaviors.justification:
MOCK-MISS: Duplicate prevention spans checkpoint/state persistence, write idempotency, and recovery path across restart. REAL-INFRA: Restart semantics are process/OS behavior; correctness also depends on real DB constraints/transactions. MOCK-HIDE: In-memory fakes can miss commit ordering, transaction boundaries, and crash-recovery edge cases.
Slot A (evidence quote): "not duplicated if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch".
Slot B (boundary signal): Requires cross-run durability and DB state consistency after interruption/restart.
Slot C (adjacent-lower miss-proof): Integration in one process can pass while real restart replays rows due to persisted offset/checkpoint mismatch.
Final label rationale: System is required by Slot B and Slot C.
REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Integration because behavior explicitly includes restart mid-batch, which implies process lifecycle and persisted recovery semantics beyond single-run module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline against a real PostgreSQL instance, force interruption mid-batch (terminate process after partial commit), restart pipeline, then assert final table contains exactly one copy per source row (via unique key/count checks).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test intentionally exercises process restart and durable state, requiring System level.
